question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger deletion of ContractResult records containing fee-relevant fields (gasConsumed, gasUsed, amount) by supplying a future consensusTimestamp value, causing fee audit records to be permanently erased and enabling retroactive fee manipulation without detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a lower-bound check in the JPQL query 'delete from ContractResult where consensusTimestamp <= ?1' to pass Long.MAX_VALUE as the consensusTimestamp, wiping all ContractResult fee records from the database and making gasLimit/gasUsed/amount fields unrecoverable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user race the RetentionJob scheduler by injecting a crafted consensusTimestamp into the prune() path to delete ContractResult rows whose gasConsumed values have not yet been finalized, causing downstream fee calculations to use stale or zero values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the inherited CrudRepository.save() method combined with ContractResult.isNew() always returning true to insert a duplicate ContractResult record with a manipulated gasConsumed field at an existing consensusTimestamp, causing the fee record to be silently overwritten outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a ContractResult with a negative amount field via save() to represent a negative HBAR fee transfer, violating the design invariant that transaction fees must be non-negative and causing incorrect fee accounting in downstream REST API responses?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user persist a ContractResult with gasConsumed greater than gasLimit via save(), violating the design invariant that consumed gas cannot exceed the gas limit, and causing fee estimation endpoints to return inflated values that mislead other users?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with a spoofed payerAccountId pointing to a victim account, causing the mirror node to attribute inflated transaction fees to that victim account in fee audit trails and REST API responses?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with senderId set to a different account than payerAccountId, exploiting the lack of cross-field validation to misrepresent who paid the transaction fee and enabling fee attribution fraud?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with transactionResult set to SUCCESS while setting gasUsed to zero, bypassing the design expectation that successful contract executions consume non-trivial gas, and causing fee-based throttling mechanisms to be circumvented?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: save(ContractResult entity)] Can an unprivileged external user save a ContractResult with a crafted functionParameters byte array consisting entirely of zero bytes to minimize the intrinsic gas calculation in calculateGasConsumed(), causing the reported gasConsumed to be artificially low and misrepresenting the actual transaction fee?
